2605.22902.title: Transcoders Trace Visual Grounding and Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
2605.22902.paper_summary: Generative Vision-Language Models (VLMs) perform well on multimodal reasoning, but how visual inputs are transformed to text remains poorly understood. Existing interpretability work on VLMs uses Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs), which decompose static residual representations and miss the functional updates that drive cross-modal interaction. We adopt a function-centric framework based on Transcoders, sparse approximations of MLP sublayers that act as a causal proxy for layer-wise computation. Applied to Gemma 3-4B-IT, the framework decomposes the model into interpretable computational pathways linking image patches to directions in token generation. Transcoder attributions produce stronger and more stable effects on visually grounded tokens under patch ablation than SAE attributions, and align better with semantically relevant image regions. A False Visual Grounding counterfactual analysis confirms that the recovered pathways are specific to vision-language interaction.Finally, we perform a structural analysis of hallucinated generations, by extracting graph-based indicators from circuit traces produced by the transcoders. A logistic classifier over these mechanistic graph features predicts hallucinations at AUC $0.68$. These results show that function-centric circuit decomposition yields interpretable and predictive accounts of multimodal computation in VLMs.
2605.22902.paper_summary_zh: 生成式視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在多模態推理上表現良好，但視覺輸入如何轉換為文本仍然不甚了解。現有對VLMs的可解釋性研究使用稀疏自編碼器（SAEs），這些自編碼器分解靜態殘差表示，卻忽略了驅動跨模態互動的功能性更新。我們採用一個基於轉碼器的功能中心框架，轉碼器是MLP子層的稀疏近似，作為層級計算的因果代理。應用於Gemma 3-4B-IT，該框架將模型分解為可解釋的計算路徑，將圖像區塊與標記生成中的方向鏈接起來。在區塊消融下，轉碼器的歸因對視覺基礎標記產生了比SAE歸因更強且更穩定的效果，並且與語義相關的圖像區域對齊得更好。一項虛假視覺基礎的反事實分析確認，恢復的路徑是特定於視覺-語言互動的。最後，我們通過從轉碼器產生的電路跡象中提取基於圖形的指標，對幻覺生成進行結構分析。對這些機械圖形特徵的邏輯回歸分類器預測幻覺的AUC為$0.68$。這些結果顯示，功能中心的電路分解提供了對VLMs中多模態計算的可解釋和可預測的解釋。
2605.22902.author: Dimitrios Damianos et.al.
2605.22902.authors: Dimitrios Damianos, Leon Voukoutis, Georgios Skyrianos, Vassilis Katsouros, Georgios Paraskevopoulos
